name: none
description: This condition is empty. It will always evaluate to true.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

name: bring_object_to_area
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object has been brought into the specified area.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be brought into the area.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area the object must be brought to.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: bring_object_to_object
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object has been brought to the location of another object.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be brought to the target object.

attributes.name: next_object
attributes.description: The target object the unit must be brought to.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: own_objects
description: This condition can be used to check if a player owns at least the specified quantity of objects matching the given filters.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of matching objects the player must own.

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.description: The specific unit type to check for.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose object count is checked.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The unit class filter. Only units of this class are counted.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The unit type filter. Only units of this type are counted.

attributes.name: include_changeable_weapon_objects
attributes.description: When enabled, also counts changeable weapon objects.

name: own_fewer_objects
description: This condition can be used to check if a player owns fewer than the specified quantity of objects matching the given filters.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The exclusive upper bound on the number of matching objects the player may own.

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.description: The specific unit type to check for.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose object count is checked.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area to restrict the count to. When not set, the entire map is checked.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The unit class filter. Only units of this class are counted.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The unit type filter. Only units of this type are counted.

attributes.name: include_changeable_weapon_objects
attributes.description: When enabled, also counts changeable weapon objects.

name: objects_in_area
description: This condition can be used to check if at least the specified number of objects matching the given filters are present in an area.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of matching objects that must be present in the area.

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.description: The specific unit type to check for.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must own the objects. When not set, objects from any player are counted.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area to check for objects.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The unit class filter. Only units of this class are counted.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The unit type filter. Only units of this type are counted.

attributes.name: object_state
attributes.description: The state units must be in to be counted.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

attributes.name: include_changeable_weapon_objects
attributes.description: When enabled, also counts changeable weapon objects.

name: destroy_object
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object has been destroyed.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be destroyed.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: capture_object
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific player has captured a specific object.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must have been captured.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must have captured the object.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: accumulate_attribute
description: This condition can be used to check if a player has accumulated at least the specified amount of a player attribute (such as a resource).

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum amount of the attribute the player must have accumulated.

attributes.name: attribute
attributes.description: The player attribute to check (e.g. food, wood, gold, stone).

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose attribute amount is checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: research_technology
description: This condition can be used to check if a player has finished researching a specific technology.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must have researched the technology.

attributes.name: technology
attributes.description: The technology that must have been researched.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: timer
description: This condition can be used to trigger after a set number of seconds have elapsed since the scenario started.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: The number of in-game seconds that must have elapsed since the scenario started.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_selected
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently selected by the (local) player.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be selected.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: ai_signal
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific AI signal has been sent.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: ai_signal
attributes.description: The AI Signal ID that must have been sent.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: player_defeated
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific player has been defeated.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player that must have been defeated.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_has_target
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently targeting another object matching the given filters.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be targeting the specified target.

attributes.name: next_object
attributes.description: The specific target object the unit must be targeting. When not set, any target matching the other filters will satisfy the condition.

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.description: The type of unit the target must be.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The class the target must belong to.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The type the target must be.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_visible
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently visible to the (local) player.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be visible.

name: object_not_visible
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently not visible to the (local) player.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must not be visible.

name: researching_tech
description: This condition can be used to check if a player is currently in the process of researching a specific technology.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must currently be researching the technology.

attributes.name: technology
attributes.description: The technology that must currently be in research.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: units_garrisoned
description: This condition can be used to check if at least the specified number of units are garrisoned inside a specific object.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of units that must be garrisoned inside the object.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object (e.g. building or transport) that must have units garrisoned inside it.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: difficulty_level
description: This condition can be used to check the current difficulty level of the scenario.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The difficulty level to check against.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: chance
description: This condition can be used to trigger with a specified percentage chance each time the trigger is evaluated.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The percentage chance (0–100) that this condition evaluates to true.

name: technology_state
description: This condition can be used to check the state of a specific technology for a player (e.g. not available, available, being researched, or researched).

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The technology state to check against.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose technology state is checked.

attributes.name: technology
attributes.description: The technology whose state is checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: variable_value
description: This condition can be used to compare a variable's current value against a quantity using a comparison operator.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The value to compare the variable against.

attributes.name: variable
attributes.description: The variable whose value is compared.

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to apply between the variable value and the quantity.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_hp
description: This condition can be used to compare the current hit points of a specific object against a value using a comparison operator.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The hit point value to compare the object's current HP against.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object whose current HP is checked.

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to apply between the object's HP and the quantity.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: diplomacy_state
description: This condition can be used to check the diplomacy stance of one player toward another.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The diplomacy stance to check for.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose stance toward the target player is checked.

attributes.name: target_player
attributes.description: The player whose stance is being evaluated from the source player's perspective.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: script_call
description: This condition can be used to call an XS script function that returns a boolean value.
The condition evaluates to true when the function returns true.

attributes.name: xs_function
attributes.description: The name of the XS script function to call. The function must return a bool.

name: object_selected_multiplayer
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently selected by a specific player in a multiplayer game.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be selected.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must have the object selected.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_visible_multiplayer
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently visible to a specific player in a multiplayer game.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be visible.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the object must be visible.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_has_action
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific object is currently performing a specific AI action, optionally targeting another object.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The object that must be performing the action.

attributes.name: next_object
attributes.description: The specific target object of the action. When not set, any target matching the other filters will satisfy the condition.

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.description: The unit type the target of the action must be.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The class the target of the action must belong to.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The type the target of the action must be.

attributes.name: unit_ai_action
attributes.description: The AI action the object must currently be performing.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: or
description: This condition acts as an OR separator between groups of conditions.
All conditions before this OR (since the last OR/AND or the start of the list) are evaluated as a group.
The trigger fires if any group evaluates to true.

name: ai_signal_multiplayer
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific AI signal has been sent in a multiplayer game.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: ai_signal
attributes.description: The AI Signal ID that must have been sent.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: building_is_trading
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific building is currently trading (i.e. has an active trade route).

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The building that must be trading.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: display_timer_triggered
description: This condition can be used to check if a display timer has been triggered (i.e. has reached zero).

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: timer_id
attributes.description: The ID of the display timer that must have triggered.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: victory_timer
description: This condition can be used to compare the remaining time on a victory timer for a player against a value using a comparison operator.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The time value to compare the victory timer against.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose victory timer is checked.

attributes.name: victory_timer_type
attributes.description: The type of victory timer to check.

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to apply between the victory timer value and the quantity.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: and
description: This condition acts as an AND separator between groups of conditions.
All conditions before this AND (since the last OR/AND or the start of the list) must all evaluate to true for the group to pass.
The trigger fires only if all groups pass.

name: decision_triggered
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific option was chosen for a decision prompt.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: decision_id
attributes.description: The ID of the decision whose chosen option is checked.

attributes.name: decision_option
attributes.description: The decision option that must have been chosen.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: object_attacked
description: This condition can be used to check if any objects matching the given filters have been attacked.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: A specific object that must have been attacked. When not set, any matching object is checked.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of matching objects that must have been attacked.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who owns the objects that must have been attacked.

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.description: The specific unit type that must have been attacked.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The unit class filter. Only objects of this class are considered.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The unit type filter. Only objects of this type are considered.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: hero_power_cast
description: This condition can be used to check if a hero power was cast by a specific player.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must have cast a hero power.

name: compare_variables
description: This condition can be used to compare the values of two variables against each other using a comparison operator.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: variable
attributes.description: The first variable to compare.

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to apply between the two variable values.

attributes.name: variable2
attributes.description: The second variable to compare against.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: trigger_active
description: This condition can be used to check if a specific trigger is currently active (enabled).

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: trigger_id
attributes.description: The trigger that must be active.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.

name: local_tech_researched
description: This condition can be used to check if a local technology has been researched.
The research is looked up on a specific unit or in an area, for a given player.

attributes.name: condition_type
attributes.description: The ID corresponding to this condition type.

attributes.name: local_technology
attributes.description: The local technology that must have been researched.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the local technology must have been researched.

attributes.name: unit_object
attributes.description: The specific unit to check for the local technology. When not set, any unit in the area is checked.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area to search for units with the local technology researched. When not set, the entire map is checked.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of units with the local technology researched.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition.
